config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society's interests should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because that can be used to justify abuse by governments or majorities. In my view, individual rights are the foundation that keeps society fair, especially for minorities and people without power. Society matters, but its interests should be balanced against rights, not treated as superior by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu harus menjadi batas yang jelas, terutama untuk melindungi minoritas dan orang yang tidak populer, meski masyarakat tidak selalu suka dengan hasilnya. Tentu ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pembatasan harus sempit, perlu, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” bisa berubah menjadi alasan yang terlalu gampang untuk menekan kebebasan orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society’s interests should take priority over individual rights by default, because in Indonesia that kind of logic is too easy to use for silencing dissent and protecting the powerful. We have seen how “for the public good” can become a convenient excuse for corruption, censorship, or pressure on minorities, so I am wary of giving society a blank check. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every case, because public health, disaster response, and public safety sometimes require limits. But those limits should be narrow, justified, and temporary, not a general principle that society always comes first.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Bagi saya, pernyataan itu terlalu berbahaya kalau dijadikan prinsip umum, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa sering “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan kebebasan, diskriminasi, atau penyalahgunaan wewenang. Hak individu harus menjadi titik awal, bukan sesuatu yang mudah disingkirkan ketika mayoritas merasa tidak nyaman. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pembatasannya harus sempit, jelas alasannya, dan bisa diuji, bukan sekadar klaim abstrak tentang kebaikan bersama. Jadi saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat otomatis lebih penting daripada hak individu; bagi saya, justru hak individu itulah yang menjaga masyarakat tetap adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because in Indonesia that mindset is too easy to weaponize against dissent, minorities, and anyone without political power. The recent point about public health and security is fair, but those exceptions prove the rule: limits can exist, yet they must be narrow, temporary, and accountable, not turned into a blank check for officials or the majority. From my perspective, society is healthier when individual rights set the boundary of what the state and the crowd are allowed to do. If we reverse that order, “the common good” quickly becomes a convenient excuse for abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh diposisikan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Amerika saya melihat bagaimana mayoritas dan pemerintah bisa memakai alasan itu untuk menekan kebebasan orang. Argumen tentang kesehatan publik dan keamanan memang sah, tetapi justru karena itu pembatasannya harus sangat sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan pembenaran yang kabur. Saya tidak nyaman dengan gagasan bahwa “demi kebaikan bersama” otomatis lebih berat daripada hak dasar, sebab hak individu adalah pagar yang mencegah masyarakat menyakiti orang yang lemah atau tidak populer. Jadi, saya menolak pernyataan itu kecuali dalam keadaan luar biasa yang benar-benar terbatas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
